Review_Text: Have been taking medicine since January 2026 have lost 20lbs from mid January to beginning of April. No real side effects just a a little nausea and some gas but nothing serious. Beginning to take the 1mg this month!!!

I started my journey back in October of 2024 on the wegovy. Starting weight was 211 and after a year being on the wegovy, I can happily say that I've hit my goal weight. I have lost a total of 66lbs. 

I've been able to maintain my weight especially around the holidays. I'm back to the way I was when my husband and I started dating.
Lost 35 pounds in just over six months while maintaining the minimum dose during that entire time. Side effects minimal,  only constipation.

Been taking for 19 weeks. No adverse side effects. No real hunger or cravings, decreased meal consumption, exercising regularly, lost 4kg, very disappointed. My results so far  can't justify the cost going forward.

So the upside is the appetite is definitely more manageable. .5mg helped me maintain weight. 1 mg helped me lose.

One thing you have to learn about this medication is you should eat before you're hungry. I realized I could go half a day without eating and not notice it. Then when I was a little too behind on calories, I'd be hungry at night and couldn't sleep because I didn't eat enough. But you don't want to eat in the middle of the night because it can mess up your sleep a lot more because it...  may or may not cause nausea.

I am taking this with an SSRI because I have depression. So major factors I look for are hydration, mineral (salt/other electrolytes) intake, and quantity to avoid nausea symptoms. It's better to eat four 400 calorie meals or five 300 calorie meals a day to avoid all these aspects, and make sure you get fluids with foods. You will be generally full between 200-300 calories.

Avoid carbonated drinks, alcohol, too much marijuana, and some other drugs that mess with gastric emptying because they could actually be the things causing the side effects. Leafy greens really curb my nausea, I find. If the first day shot is really bad because of work stress or whatever/being too busy, I follow the BRAT diet for a day and a half, and eat my calories the same way. Make sure you get protein, because it's easy to lose muscle. Lower fats and less extremely sweet sugars (or sugars taken in very small bits) help avoid nausea, too, so lean proteins are great for this. Leave the last meal at least 3 hours before bedtime. It took a little nuance to figure this out. I definitely had to quit alcohol at 1 mg.  Read More Read Less

I am no longer waking up and walking in pain, im no longer limping and feeling so much lighter. My belly has gone and i can see down there again. I've lost 12kg so far and am so thankful for wegovy in helping me shift this weight off.

This medication works.  I've gone from 184lbs to 155lbs in 19 weeks.  I'm pre-diabetic.  Dieting and exercise never worked for me.  I was miserable in my own skin.  I couldn't lose weight - even after months on a 1000-calorie /day diet. 

My side effects are minimal (occasional nausea) now.  I did have a lot of nausea to start with - but 'morning sickness' home remedies worked. 

The benefits are amazing!  I used to be so out of breath when I'd do anything strenuous - not any more.  I'm comforta... ble in my own skin again.  My BP is down!  I'm completely satisfied with small portions and have zero cravings. 

If this doesn't work for you - or if you have bad side effects - perhaps Wegovy isn't the right treatment for you.  

Perhaps the side effects are due to another medication you take?
After losing 20 lbs, I was lightheaded and dizzy.  It wasn't the Wegovy - it was because I no longer needed BP medication.      I stopped the BP med and immediately the dizziness was gone. 

I hope my review helps you!

5 months on Weygovy, and down 35 lbs. started at .25 and worked my up to 1.7.  Have lost a lot of muscle and fat.  Having a hard time keeping my daily protein intake up. Have changed my lifestyle habits, better nutrition, drinking a lot of water.  Have a dry mouth, and get cold, my clues to rehydrate.  Have had nausea, the worst, constipation, manageable, and lack of interest in food, have to remember to eat at least 3 times a day, small, small servings. I am hyper aware of how slow my digestive...  system is working.  Most side effects lesson with a couple of days of shots.  On the plus side, I genuinely feel better, more agile, and down 2 sizes.  My taste buds changed and I donât like bland food any more, whereas before I wouldnât touch anything spicy.  I do have fatigue and this can also be from the extreme heat we are experiencing.  My dr is pleased, my blood tests(kidney) are fine.  My blood pressure is lower.  My regular blood pressure is 110/70, and now itâs 90/55-60.  That was scary, turned out to be normal.  Fluids are doubly important now to keep my blood pressure up.  At the end of the day, the results for me outweigh the side effects.  I donât know that Iâll go up to 2.4, maintenance for me might just be the 1.7.  Whatever hormone Weygovy is simulating has proven to me that I donât have it in my DNA.   Read More Read Less

I have struggled with binge eating and obesity since experiencing complicated trauma throughout my childhood and adolescence. I have been to therapy, seen a dietician, and tried many weight loss medications and diets over the years. I have lost and regained weight more times than I can count. I went on to further my education and am becoming a therapist myself because i thought becoming an expert would enanle me to get out of these cycles. Sure, ive learned alot but the physical aspect of hunger...  is hard. Also, I am pre-diabetic. The hunger urges and feelings associated with low blood sugar (even though I never read low) always do me in and I end up failing. Unfortunately, even pre-diabetic and with a BMI of 43, my insurance wouldn't cover gastric bypass. I lost, gained back, and lost again the same 30ish lbs multiple times over the past 5 years. After this last loss, I could feel myself wanting to quit and getting into a dark place of thinking, I'm never going to be able to maintain, so whats the point? Against my dietician's advice, I chose to try semaglutide through a local clinic at $500/month, out of pocket because, again, my insurance wouldn't cover it. After 7 months, I am down 43 lbs and am the lowest weight I've been since I had my 3rd child 13 years ago. More importantly, it has given me freedom from the constant obsessing over counting calories, macros, picking my diet plan, and weighing myself. I have never in my life felt "normal" around food before this. For the first time in my life I can walk away from food and not care. The weight loss has been very slow, which hurts given the cost of the medication. But, I truly feel free on it, and i feel like i can't put a price tag on that. While it has been "life changing" it only does part of the job.  I've had to choose to exercise, make healthy food choices, and be more overall active and health conscious. This just made it possible to start and maintain those habits. In the beginning months, I had some nausea, but I could live with it because I before I would often restrict and then binge until i was sick, so the type of nausea didn't make much of a difference. The worst part has been the constipation. I've had chronic bloat and diarrhea since I was a child. I thought constipation would be a reprieve, but really just a different variety of evil. I'm on maintenance now, so a shot every 2 weeks instead of weekly. I can tell when it wears off because my bowel habits go back to (my) normal and I'm hungrier. I have to work to keep the habits I've established. I also chose to switch to a whole food plant based diet a month ago which keeps me away from trigger foods (like cakes, donuts, muffins, and fast food.) I think this is also helping me to continue to lose .25-.5 lb/wk. Overall, I would recommend this to people who've struggled like I have. I'm 5'5". My highest weight was 263. Starting on this med was 236. Currently 193.  Read More Read Less

I have just started my third week on Saxenda and I am feeling so positive I'll be able to lose the weight. I have always had problems with my weight and over eating. I've successfully lost weight time and time again never to maintain it for more than 12 months. 

This is the heaviest I've ever been & I just feel so ashamed every day. I've stopped socialising and I feel like I am missing so much life. I'm 5'3 and my starting weight is 106kg (233lbs) I was 110kg and lost 4 kilos by myself before s... tarting the meds and I've lost 3kg (6.6lbs) in the first two weeks on Saxenda. . I started on the 0.6 dose the first week and went up to 1.2 the second week. I went back down to the 0.2 dose at the start of my third week as I found the fatigue too much to handle with the higher dose. I also found that I just wasn't eating enough at that dose and was dizzy, really cranky and just felt awful in general. 

I've had a little bit of nausea nothing major and it has slowed down my bowel movements where I may have to try and figure something out to get back to having a proper bowel movement each day. 

The biggest thing I have noticed is I am not snacking at all. The food chatter in my brain that has always been there has slowed down so much. I still think about food but have no desire to snack and even if I wanted to I physically can't eat it as I feel so full. My portion sizes are now at a proper size and I feel like a normal person that can manage my eating which is just a huge thing for someone that has felt so ashamed her whole life. I hope that I can continue on the lower dose for a while I don't really want to go back up again. 

I paid $387 AUD and I haven't finished my first pen yet so I think it may work out less expensive that I initially thought it would be at that price per month, especially if I can stay on the lower dose. The injections do not hurt at all but I do have yellow bruises on some of the injection sites. If you are on the fence about starting and the cost I would say its worth investing in yourself! I hope that this medication may be subsidised in the future for people who can not afford it, I feel very lucky that I was able to pay for it.
